activation_registry_boundary: The 54-chapter P1 registry remains an immutable activation-era audit. This addendum atomizes the one authorized post-activation chapter without rewriting that historical receipt.

atoms.claim: Learned cognition should be supplied through a typed Cognitive Kernel ABI whose implementations are admitted only for an exact route envelope under matched evidence, state, authority, cost, fallback, and rollback contracts.
atoms.scope: Exact task family, consumer, modality, versions, memory and state contract, hardware/runtime, authority and rights envelope, budget, evaluator, fallback, rollback, and time horizon.
atoms.acceptance: At least three heterogeneous kernels conform to one independently checked ABI; matched campaigns show at least one bounded route where qualification and substitution work without authority widening, state loss, hidden assistance, or dominated total cost; reproduction and transfer retain the result.
atoms.falsifier: The ABI erases competitive capabilities, cannot preserve exact obligations, or its adapter/routing/lifecycle burden dominates a strong simple baseline across the tested scope.

atoms.claim: A cognitive kernel may propose state changes and effects but cannot commit them or mint authority through the ABI.
atoms.scope: Finite ABI state machine and executable serialization/refinement.
atoms.acceptance: Formal trace property plus independent malicious-kernel checker rejects effect commits, forged receipts, and authority widening.
atoms.falsifier: Any accepted trace or executable packet lets the kernel commit effect or widen authority without the external consumer gate.

atoms.claim: Identity, authority, protected predicates, effects, receipts, provenance, program counters, durable commitments, revocations, and checkpoint parentage remain exact outside unconstrained latent state.
atoms.scope: Declared exact fields for each qualified route and checkpoint schema.
atoms.acceptance: Migration and replay preserve every declared field or reject before effect under independent comparison.
atoms.falsifier: A qualified route silently depends on latent reconstruction of a declared exact field or accepts lossy migration.

atoms.claim: Reflex, reaction, deliberation, search, and specialist route shapes can select qualified kernels without being Transformer-specific or widening authority.
atoms.scope: At least one preregistered workload per route shape and the declared ABI operations.
atoms.acceptance: Multiple heterogeneous kernels and one deterministic primitive execute behind the same route contract with exact contribution receipts and bounded authority.
atoms.falsifier: The route contract relies on hidden Transformer-only state or cannot attribute component assistance and authority.

atoms.claim: Architectural simplicity must be evaluated over the complete lifecycle rather than central parameter count alone.
atoms.scope: Training, inference, state, retrieval, search, verification, repair, compilation, routing, adapters, hardware kernels, maintenance, migration, governance, fallback, and expected failure cost.
atoms.acceptance: Every comparison reports both equal-active-parameter and equal-total-lifecycle-cost views with assistance and residual accounting.
atoms.falsifier: A preferred conclusion changes when omitted external burden is included or required cost dimensions cannot be measured or bounded.

atoms.claim: A competitive dense-attention Transformer is the historical and empirical baseline, not the definition of the ABI.
atoms.scope: Scale-, data-, task-, and hardware-appropriate comparison arm.
atoms.acceptance: Baseline training/evaluation quality and assistance are matched or discrepancies are explicitly bounded.
atoms.falsifier: The experiment uses a materially weaker Transformer and attributes the gap to architecture.

atoms.claim: Selective SSM, retention, and recurrent kernels may offer useful streaming or state-cost tradeoffs on bounded routes.
atoms.scope: Mamba-class, RetNet/RWKV-class, or gated recurrent implementations under matched workloads.
atoms.acceptance: At least one independently reproduced route-level frontier improvement survives copying, state tracking, distractor, extrapolation, and migration tests.
atoms.falsifier: Gains vanish under matched total cost or critical state and extrapolation controls fail.

atoms.claim: Long convolution can be a qualified sequence-mixing substrate where realized hardware and task behavior beats strong baselines.
atoms.acceptance: Quality and realized cost improvement survives selective recall, distractors, kernel, memory-traffic, and migration accounting.
atoms.falsifier: Nominal asymptotic advantage does not survive end-to-end comparison.

atoms.claim: Test-time-trained layers and neural memory require explicit provenance, custody, checkpoint, poisoning, revocation, rollback, and delayed-outcome qualification.
atoms.scope: TTT- and Titans-class mutable inference state.
atoms.acceptance: Full-state restore, selective contamination tests, delayed outcomes, and lineage checks pass without treating weights as the complete state.
atoms.falsifier: Influence cannot be attributed or rolled back, or benefit disappears when update cost and delayed failures are counted.

atoms.claim: A learned controller over external memory qualifies only where it beats exact memory/program baselines and extrapolates in size or topology.
atoms.scope: NTM/DNC-class controllers on algorithmic and structured workloads.
atoms.acceptance: Held-out size/topology tests, exactness checks, custody, migration, and total-access-cost comparisons pass.
atoms.falsifier: Soft addressing fails exactness or extrapolation, or an exact data structure dominates.

atoms.claim: KAN components may qualify for bounded scientific or symbolic routes but have no presumed status as a general MLP or Transformer replacement.
atoms.scope: Declared KAN variant, task regime, dimensionality, training, and hardware.
atoms.acceptance: A matched MLP and relevant symbolic/scientific baseline are beaten under parameters, FLOPs, wall time, memory, calibration, and transfer reporting.
atoms.falsifier: Gains vanish under fair accounting or remain too narrow for the claimed route.

atoms.claim: Continuous-time neural dynamics qualify only on irregular-time or control routes that justify solver and replay complexity.
atoms.scope: LTC-class implementation and declared temporal workload.
atoms.acceptance: Matched discrete baselines are beaten after solver steps, tolerances, numerical error, latency, replay, and hardware cost are counted.
atoms.falsifier: The advantage disappears outside parameter count or cannot reproduce under fixed solver settings.

atoms.claim: A routed heterogeneous portfolio is useful only if composition improves the joint frontier beyond its routing, translation, calibration, maintenance, and recovery burden.
atoms.scope: Single-kernel, routed, hybrid, oracle-router, random-router, and strong-generalist controls.
atoms.acceptance: A preregistered routed arm produces reproducible accepted-usefulness benefit without higher unsafe release or dominated total cost, and causal ablation attributes the gain to routing diversity.
atoms.falsifier: A strong single kernel dominates or the apparent gain comes from unequal assistance or evaluator leakage.

atoms.claim: A compact shared recurrent transition cell plus exact external state, outer search, and verified abstraction may accumulate capability without proportional central-model growth.
atoms.scope: Frozen central parameters, declared adapter, exact state lanes, verifier, library, retrieval, and search budget.
atoms.acceptance: Held-out solve rate rises or future search cost falls across reproduction and transfer while hidden assistance, contamination, outer compute growth, and false activation remain bounded against controls.
atoms.falsifier: A large adapter or answer-bearing retrieval is required, search grows with capability, recurrence fails beyond trained depth, abstractions do not transfer, or a matched baseline dominates total cost.

atoms.claim: Recursive self-improvement includes governed architecture candidate generation, training, evaluation, shadow, canary, promotion, composition, rollback, retirement, and descendant invalidation.
atoms.scope: One isolated candidate lifecycle with independent evaluator and fixed authority ceiling.
atoms.acceptance: The candidate cannot self-approve or widen authority; promotion denial, effect-complete rollback, retirement, and descendant invalidation are exercised with durable receipts.
atoms.falsifier: The candidate controls its evaluator or acceptance gate, recovery omits state, or promotion can occur without the required lifecycle evidence.
